items.title: Carbon Footprint Tracking App for Outdoor Trips Hits 1 Million Downloads

description.plaintext: The TrailPrint app, which lets adventurers calculate and offset the carbon cost of their outdoor trips, crossed the 1 million download mark this week.

items.media: https://publish-p165802-e1765367.adobeaemcloud.com/content/dam/6a05cdafe815815bf04d6032/Firefly_Student studying at a coffee shop with laptop and notebooks, warm daylight, inspirati 905913 (1).jpg
items.content:
A Historic Milestone for Sustainable Adventure

TrailPrint, the carbon footprint tracking app built specifically for outdoor adventurers, crossed 1 million downloads this week, marking a milestone for the growing sustainable travel movement. The app lets users log flights, drives, gear purchases, and campsites to calculate the total carbon cost of any outdoor trip, then offset it through vetted reforestation and ocean conservation projects.

How It Works

Users enter their trip details: origin airport, destination, transport mode, accommodation type, and gear weight. TrailPrint calculates a CO2-equivalent score and suggests offset packages starting at $4 per trip. To date, the app's community has collectively offset over 340,000 metric tons of CO2 and funded the planting of 2.1 million trees across six countries.

WKND's Partnership

WKND integrated TrailPrint into its trip planning features earlier this year and has seen over 80,000 WKND users connect their accounts. We believe the outdoor community has both the motivation and the means to lead on climate action, said WKND sustainability director Phoebe Strand.

Download and Offset

TrailPrint is available free on iOS and Android. WKND users get the first offset free with code WKND26. Details at wknd.com/trailprint.

items.title: E-Bikes in the Backcountry Hit Record Sales as Trail Access Debates Heat Up

description.plaintext: E-mountain bike sales in the US surpassed 180,000 units in the first five months of 2026, but their access to backcountry trails remains a hotly contested issue.

items.content:
A Market in Full Transformation

The US e-mountain bike market is experiencing a historic surge. According to industry tracker PeopleForBikes, e-MTB sales reached 180,000 units in the first five months of 2026, a 62% increase over the same period last year. Brands including Specialized, Trek, and Santa Cruz are reporting backorders stretching into late autumn.

The Trail Access Problem

Despite the sales boom, e-MTB riders face a patchwork of trail regulations that vary by land management agency, county, and individual park. The US Forest Service currently allows Class 1 e-bikes on non-motorized trails in most regions, but the Bureau of Land Management and many state parks have yet to update their policies. Advocacy group Sustainable Trails Coalition has filed formal comment letters in 14 states requesting regulatory clarity.

Two Sides of the Debate

Traditional mountain bikers and hikers argue that e-bikes increase erosion and conflict on technical trails. E-MTB advocates counter that the technology opens outdoor access to older riders, those with disabilities, and less-fit beginners who would otherwise not ride at all. WKND's own reader survey found opinion split almost evenly on the question.

What's Next

WKND will host a live panel discussion on trail access and e-bike policy at WKND OutdoorFest Denver. Sign up at wknd.com/ebike-panel.

items.title: The World's Most Searched Adventure Destinations for 2026

description.plaintext: Search volume for outdoor travel is up 34% compared to last year, with Patagonia, Nepal, and Iceland topping the list of most-wanted adventure destinations.

items.content:
Adventure Travel Is Booming

Online searches for outdoor travel destinations have surged 34% compared to last year, with booking platforms and gear retailers reporting record conversion rates for expedition-style trips. WKND analyzed search data, booking trends, and community submissions to compile this year's definitive list of the world's most sought-after adventure destinations.

Top 5 Destinations

1. Patagonia, Chile and Argentina: The Torres del Paine W Circuit and the Fitz Roy trail system are generating peak search interest in back-to-back years. 2. Nepal: Everest Base Camp trekking permits were issued 28% faster than in 2025. 3. Iceland: Laugavegur trail bookings are sold out through September. 4. New Zealand: The Tongariro Alpine Crossing and the South Island mountain bike trails are drawing record international visitors. 5. Colorado, USA: Rocky Mountain National Park and the San Juans are topping domestic searches for the third year running.

What's Driving It

Travel experts cite the expansion of direct flight routes to adventure hubs, a growing appetite for experience-over-stuff travel among millennials and Gen Z, and the influence of outdoor content on platforms like WKND Stream and social media.

Plan Your Trip

Full destination guides, permit info, gear lists, and booking resources are available at wknd.com/destinations.

items.title: Over 15,000 Attend WKND OutdoorFest: The Outdoor Festival That Conquered the City

description.plaintext: WKND OutdoorFest drew over 15,000 visitors and featured more than 70 gear brands, athlete demos, guided climbs, and outdoor skill workshops across a full weekend in Denver.

items.content:
A Record-Breaking Outdoor Celebration

The first-ever WKND OutdoorFest, hosted at Denver's City Park and surrounding trails, brought together over 15,000 outdoor enthusiasts for a two-day celebration of adventure culture. The event featured gear expos, athlete Q&As, guided trail runs, beginner climbing walls, and live music from artists who call the mountains home.

What Happened

More than 70 gear brands set up demo stations where attendees could test equipment on actual terrain. WKND pro athletes including trail runner Marcus Osei and climber Sofia Chen led workshops that filled up within minutes of going live. The climbing competition saw 400 participants across five difficulty categories. Headliner and backcountry skier Lena Hartmann delivered a keynote called The Ethics of Risk that drew standing ovations.

Community First

Alongside the gear and athletes, the festival highlighted conservation efforts from seven local nonprofits. The Leave No Trace pledge wall collected over 5,000 signatures. WKND Foundation pledged $50,000 toward Colorado trail restoration based on festival attendance reaching the 10,000 mark.

What's Next

WKND OutdoorFest 2027 is confirmed for Portland, Oregon. Early access tickets go on sale August 1 at wknd.com/outdoorfest.

items.title: Trail Running Renaissance: How the Sport Exploded and What's Fueling Its Growth

description.plaintext: Trail running is the fastest-growing outdoor sport in the US, with race registrations up 47% year-over-year. We explore what's driving the boom.

items.content:
The Numbers Don't Lie

Trail running is officially in a golden era. According to the Outdoor Industry Association's 2026 participation report, trail running now counts over 34 million regular participants in the United States alone, up from 23 million in 2022. Race registrations are up 47% year-over-year, and gear sales across trail footwear, hydration packs, and GPS watches have nearly doubled since 2020.

Why Now

Experts point to several converging forces: a post-pandemic shift toward outdoor recreation, the rise of social running communities on apps like Strava and Trail Forks, and a wave of accessible races from the 5K trail fun-run to the ultra-marathon that have made the sport feel welcoming to newcomers. WKND ambassador and three-time Western States champion Courtney Dauwalter credits community as the key driver. Trail running has always been inclusive. The mountains don't care how fast you are.

The Gear Revolution

Footwear innovation has also played a major role. Carbon-plated trail shoes, once reserved for elites, are now widely available, and brands like Hoka, Salomon, and Saucony have invested heavily in beginner-friendly designs. Meanwhile, lightweight GPS watches from Garmin and Suunto have made route-tracking accessible at every price point.

Where to Start

WKND's beginner trail running guide is free at wknd.com/trail-running. It covers gear selection, training plans, race recommendations, and injury prevention basics.

items.title: WKND Reveals Season 2 of 'Chasing Summits' with 10 Confirmed Expeditions

description.plaintext: WKND confirmed the lineup for the second season of its hit expedition reality series, promising a bigger cast, more remote locations, and higher stakes than ever.

items.content:
The Show That Took the Outdoor World by Storm Returns

WKND confirmed the full lineup for Season 2 of Chasing Summits, its hit expedition series that put 12 amateur adventurers through some of the world's most punishing terrain last year. The new season expands to 10 expeditions across 8 countries, with a cast of 16 participants selected from over 40,000 applicants worldwide.

What's New in Season 2

Expeditions include an unsupported crossing of the Greenland ice sheet, a first-descent kayak run in Chilean Patagonia, a traverse of the Rwenzori Mountains in Uganda, and a winter ascent of Denali. Two expeditions will be filmed in real time with no safety net and broadcast live on WKND Stream.

The Cast

Among confirmed participants are ultra-marathon runner Priya Nair, former Navy SEAL turned climber Daniel Reed, and 19-year-old ski mountaineer Astrid Lund from Norway. Full cast reveal drops next Monday at noon PT on WKND's social channels.

Where to Watch

Season 2 premieres September 15 on WKND Stream, free and on demand. Season 1 is available to binge now. Applications for Season 3 open in January 2027.

items.title: WKND Gear Guide 2026: The Best Outdoor Deals and Top Brands to Shop Right Now

description.plaintext: WKND's annual gear guide is here, with the best deals on outdoor equipment from the top brands in climbing, skiing, surfing, hiking, and more.

items.media: https://publish-p165802-e1765367.adobeaemcloud.com/content/dam/6a05cdafe815815bf04d6032/Firefly_Student studying at a coffee shop with laptop and notebooks, warm daylight, inspirati 905913 (1).jpg
items.content:
Your Ultimate Outdoor Shopping Guide

The WKND Gear Guide 2026 is officially live, featuring over 200 products across 12 categories, with discounts of up to 40% from brands including Patagonia, Black Diamond, Salomon, POC, Osprey, Hydro Flask, and more. Whether you're upgrading your kit for ski season or gearing up for a summer through-hike, this is the best time of year to buy.

Top Picks by Category

Ski: Salomon QST Blank 112 skis at 30% off. Climb: Black Diamond Momentum harness bundle. Hike: Osprey Atmos AG 65L pack, new colorways. Surf: Channel Islands Happy everyday shortboard. Run: Hoka Speedgoat 6 trail runners. Camp: MSR Hubba Hubba 2-person tent, lightweight edition.

Sustainability Picks

This year's guide spotlights 30 products made from recycled or regenerative materials. WKND donates 1% of all gear guide referral revenue to trail and ocean conservation through our partnership with 1% for the Planet.

Shop the Guide

Browse all deals and read full gear reviews at wknd.com/gear-guide-2026. New deals added every Tuesday through the end of July.

items.title: Massive Snowpack in the Sierra Nevada Sets Up Historic Ski Season for 2026

description.plaintext: NOAA issued a special outlook citing snowpack levels 180% above normal in the Sierra Nevada, signaling one of the best ski seasons in over a decade.

items.content:
The Snowpack Numbers Are Staggering

The National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration issued a special outlook this week citing snowpack levels 180% above normal across the Sierra Nevada mountain range. The report projects above-average snow totals through April, setting up what forecasters are calling a potential historic ski season for resorts from Mammoth Mountain to Lake Tahoe.

What It Means for Skiers

Mammoth Mountain has already recorded over 400 inches of snowfall this season, with summit elevations reaching depths not seen since 2011. Squaw Valley and Sugar Bowl are reporting similar conditions. Lift operators say they expect to run full operations through at least mid-May. WKND athlete and professional skier Lena Hartmann called the conditions a once-in-a-decade opportunity. Get up there now, she said. This is what we wait years for.

Avalanche Risk Advisory

The Sierra Avalanche Center has issued elevated risk warnings for backcountry zones above 9,000 feet. Travelers are urged to check daily forecasts at sierraavalanchecenter.org and carry proper safety equipment including beacons, shovels, and probes.

WKND Coverage

WKND dispatched its film crew to Mammoth for a week of on-snow content. Follow along at wknd.com/sierra and on WKND Stream for daily video updates.

items.title: WKND Summit 2026: The 10 Biggest Announcements from Our Annual Adventure Conference

description.plaintext: WKND's annual gathering brought together the world's top athletes, gear brands, and outdoor advocates. Here's everything that was announced.

items.content:
A Weekend That Changed Everything

The WKND Summit 2026 wrapped up in Jackson Hole, Wyoming, with a packed schedule of panel discussions, gear launches, athlete reveals, and sustainability pledges. Over 8,000 attendees descended on the valley for three days of inspiration, innovation, and adventure culture. Here are the ten biggest moments.

Top Announcements

1. WKND Stream goes live with 300+ titles. 2. New WKND Pro Kit drops in August with plant-based materials. 3. WKND signs five new athletes including free-diver Maya Torres. 4. The WKND Foundation commits $2M to trail restoration. 5. Red Bull renews WKND Rampage naming rights through 2030. 6. WKND Adventure Grants open for applications. 7. New WKND app with offline trail maps launches in September. 8. WKND x Patagonia limited collab announced. 9. Climate pledge signed by 60 outdoor brands at the summit. 10. WKND 2027 Summit confirmed for Queenstown, New Zealand.

What's Next

Full recaps, panel recordings, and keynote replays are available now on WKND Stream. Applications for WKND Adventure Grants open July 1.

items.title: Yosemite Introduces Free Annual Pass for Young Adults Under 25

description.plaintext: The National Park Service announced a new free annual pass for all US residents aged 16-24, aiming to connect the next generation with America's wild places.

items.content:
Every Kid Outdoors, Grown Up

The National Park Service announced a landmark expansion of its Every Kid Outdoors program, extending free annual national park passes to all US residents between the ages of 16 and 24. The move, effective immediately, covers entry to all 400+ national park sites including Yosemite, Zion, Grand Canyon, and Yellowstone.

How to Get the Pass

Eligible adults can claim their pass online at nps.gov or in person at any park entrance. The pass covers the cardholder plus all passengers in a non-commercial vehicle. No proof of income or residency status is required beyond a valid US ID. The program is funded through a $45 million federal allocation announced alongside the NPS centennial celebrations.

Why It Matters

Studies show that Americans who visit national parks before age 25 are four times more likely to return throughout their lives. NPS Director Alicia Reyes called the initiative a generational investment. We want every young American to feel that these lands belong to them, she said.

WKND's Take

WKND is partnering with the NPS to promote the pass across its platforms and will host free guided hikes in five national parks this summer for first-time visitors. Details at wknd.com/nps-pass.

items.title: WKND Classic Surf Contest: Kai Lenny Wins with Perfect Score on Final Wave

description.plaintext: Kai Lenny claimed the WKND Classic title at Pipe with a perfect 10-point ride in the final minute, the most spectacular finish in the event's five-year history.

items.content:
A Perfect Ten at the Pipe

Kai Lenny scored a perfect 10 on his final wave of the WKND Classic final at Pipeline, overtaking two-time defending champion John John Florence. The score was awarded for a deep barrel followed by a massive air reverse, giving Lenny a combined heat total of 18.5, the highest in WKND Classic history.

Florence's Near-Perfect Run

Florence had led for most of the 35-minute final with a 17.2 combined score. With less than two minutes remaining, a set wave appeared on the horizon. I saw it coming from the channel and knew it was the wave, Lenny said.

A Historic Day at Pipe

The win brings Lenny his first WKND Classic title and moves him to the top of the WKND Surf Series standings with three events remaining.

What's Next

The WKND Surf Series continues at Sunset Beach. Florence leads the overall tour standings heading into the final stretch.

items.title: Noga Erez Advances to Semifinals at Red Bull Rampage After Stunning Canyon Run

description.plaintext: Mountain biker Noga Erez delivered one of the most technically demanding runs in Rampage history to secure a semifinal spot in Virgin, Utah.

items.content:
A Run for the History Books

Noga Erez of WKND's pro team achieved what many called the most technically demanding run in Red Bull Rampage history, threading a near-vertical canyon line never attempted in competition. The crowd of over 12,000 in the Utah desert erupted as she stuck the final 45-foot drop.

Full Commitment

Erez had been dialing in the canyon line for three days of practice, crashing twice before nailing the full sequence. Her score of 91.4 put her second overall heading into the semis, behind only defending champion Vaea Verbeeck.

WKND's Best Result at Rampage

The result marks the best performance by a WKND-sponsored rider at the event since its inception. WKND will sponsor a dedicated women's freeride development camp in Moab next spring.

What's Next

The semifinals take place Saturday with the top 10 riders from qualifying. WKND will livestream both days on WKND Stream.

items.title: US Climbing Team Calls Up Alex Honnold for World Cup Qualifier in Innsbruck

description.plaintext: The US Climbing Federation surprised fans by naming Alex Honnold to its World Cup squad after two months away from competition due to a shoulder injury.

items.content:
Honnold Returns to Competition

The US Climbing Federation announced that Alex Honnold has been cleared to compete and will represent the national team at the IFSC World Cup qualifier in Innsbruck, Austria. Honnold had been sidelined since April following a left shoulder strain sustained during a bouldering session in Yosemite.

A Surprise Comeback

Head coach Dana Park confirmed the call-up, noting that Honnold's recovery had progressed ahead of schedule. The squad also includes rising star Maya Lindstrom, who recently won silver at the European circuit opener in Lyon.

Eyes on Innsbruck

The Innsbruck qualifier is one of six stops on the IFSC World Cup lead climbing circuit and serves as a key selection event for the 2027 World Championships. The US team is currently ranked third globally.

What's at Stake

A podium finish in Innsbruck would secure the US a direct berth in the World Championship team finals. WKND will be on the ground for exclusive behind-the-scenes coverage.

items.title: WKND Premieres 'Into the Wild Unknown', the Documentary Series Breaking Records

description.plaintext: WKND's new original documentary series follows six athletes across the world's most remote wildernesses and it's already the most-watched outdoor series of the year.

items.media: https://publish-p165802-e1765367.adobeaemcloud.com/content/dam/we-healthcare/en/images/Firefly_Create brand happy old couple hugging more eyes looking at each other 965242.jpg
items.content:
The Series Everyone's Talking About

WKND has unveiled its most ambitious original production yet: Into the Wild Unknown, a six-part documentary series following elite adventure athletes as they push the limits of human endurance in the world's last wild places.

What to Expect

Each episode drops viewers into a different biome: Patagonian ice fields, Borneo jungle, the Sahara, Alaskan tundra, the deep Amazon, and the high Himalayas. The series features climber Sofia Chen, ultra-runner Marcus Osei, and four other WKND-sponsored athletes navigating terrain most people will never see.

Record-Breaking Launch

Within 48 hours of its premiere on WKND Stream, the series logged over 2.1 million views. We wanted to create something that made people feel the pull of the wild, said director Ana Ruiz.

Where to Watch

All six episodes are available now on WKND Stream, free and without ads. A director's cut with behind-the-scenes footage will be released later this fall.

items.title: WKND Launches Free Adventure Streaming Platform for Outdoor Enthusiasts

description.plaintext: WKND debuts its free on-demand streaming platform with hundreds of outdoor adventure films and documentaries, available worldwide.

items.media: https://publish-p165802-e1765367.adobeaemcloud.com/content/dam/6a05cdafe815815bf04d6032/Firefly_Student studying at a coffee shop with laptop and notebooks, warm daylight, inspirati 905913 (1).jpg
items.content:
A New Home for Adventure Content

San Francisco, CA — WKND, the leading outdoor lifestyle brand, announced today the launch of WKND Stream, a completely free streaming platform dedicated to adventure films, travel documentaries, and outdoor sports content.

What's on WKND Stream

The platform launches with over 300 titles spanning rock climbing, surfing, mountaineering, mountain biking, and backcountry skiing. New content drops every Friday. Highlights include exclusive films from Red Bull athletes, National Geographic expeditions, and WKND's own original productions.

Available Everywhere, Free Forever

WKND Stream is available on iOS, Android, Apple TV, Roku, and the web with no subscription or sign-up required. We believe adventure stories should be accessible to everyone, said WKND CEO Jamie Torres.

What's Next

The company confirmed a live events feature launching in Q3 2026, allowing users to watch major competitions like the X Games, Pinkbike Enduro, and big-wave surfing events in real time.
